candidates.direction_name: Restore Grouped Rows From 2 Back To 4 On The Compact Two-Stage PTX Ring
ranking_notes: This is the highest-confidence next move because it directly targets the one metric that snapped back hardest on the compact two-stage surface: long_scoreboard.
It preserves the recovered 202-register budget and 22,016 B shared-memory footprint instead of reopening broader pipeline surgery.
If it still cannot beat the current 25.46 ms accepted base, the compact PTX family is likely plateaued and the next branch should be either barrier trim or 256x128.
candidates.hypothesis: The grouped_rows=2 retest kept the recovered register budget but clearly reintroduced the long_scoreboard problem on the compact two-stage surface. Restoring grouped_rows to 4 should recover the better row-order reuse pattern from the earlier PTX anchor without reopening the losing round-6 split logic.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Long-scoreboard pressure from the grouped_rows=2 launch order is now the most specific local bottleneck to remove.
candidates.risk: Low. This is a single-parameter launch-order restore on the currently healthy compact two-stage PTX surface.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if restoring grouped_rows to 4 requires any change beyond the hot-band launch-order constant and its immediate grouping math.
expected_local_changes: Leave the compact two-stage PTX ring unchanged.
Re-measure whether the long_scoreboard rebound disappears without giving back the recovered register budget.

candidates.direction_name: Trim The Compact Two-Stage PTX Wait-Sync Cadence Without Growing Shared Memory
ranking_notes: This is the best local follow-up once grouped_rows is back at 4 because it attacks the remaining sync tax without changing stage depth.
It avoids the 30,464 B shared-memory blowup and 254-register failure mode from the round-6 split.
It stays below dir_01 because the current run is still more specifically explained by launch-order damage than by residual barrier cost.
candidates.hypothesis: Once grouped_rows is restored, the remaining local gap on the compact 22,016 B PTX surface should again be CTA handoff overhead rather than launch-order damage. A narrow wait/sync cadence trim on the compact two-stage ring could reduce barrier stalls without reintroducing the shared-memory or register blowups seen in the larger stage experiments.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier and CTA handoff overhead are the likely next bottlenecks after grouped_rows is restored.
candidates.risk: Moderate. This is still hot-loop PTX synchronization surgery, but it stays on the compact 22,016 B shared-memory surface rather than reopening the failed staged branch.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the edit requires increasing shared memory above 22,016 B or materially increasing registers beyond the current 202-register budget.
expected_local_changes: Retune the compact two-stage wait_group and __syncthreads cadence without changing stage depth.
Keep the PTX microkernel on the 22,016 B shared-memory budget.
Do not change grouped_rows again in the same implementation.

candidates.direction_name: Reopen The 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Branch From The Compact PTX Base
ranking_notes: This remains the highest-ceiling structural branch after the local PTX launch-order work is exhausted.
The writer-safe 256x128 path is already present in the tree, so the reopen is bounded even though it is still risky.
It stays rank 3 because the immediate evidence still points to a smaller launch-order correction first.
candidates.hypothesis: If the compact PTX branch remains stuck near 25 ms even after grouped_rows is restored, the geometry ceiling is probably real. The repo already contains a writer-safe 256x128 / 64x64 path aligned with the persistent human guidance, so that structural branch should remain on deck.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: The 128x128 PTX surface may still be occupancy-limited by geometry and warp reuse even after local launch-order cleanup.
candidates.risk: High. This is a broader structural reopen that touches geometry, ownership, and export path together.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the reopen requires reviving a previously broken ownership or export path instead of the writer-safe 256x128 surface already present in the source.
expected_local_changes: Re-enable the writer-safe 256x128 / 64x64 PTX hot-band branch.
Keep async-copy staging and shared-memory reuse intact on the larger CTA geometry.
Leave non-hot-band routing unchanged in the first pass.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
